feed.title: HA News Feed — Pokémon GO, Brawl Stars & AI
feed.subtitle: Aggregated news: Pokémon GO, Brawl Stars & AI

entry.title: ⚡ Pokémon GO Trainer Battle Update
⚡ Keep the festivities going with the Special Anniversary Pikachu Celebration event!
⚡ Wish Professor Willow a very happy birthday with Professor Willow’s assistant Pikachu!
⚡ Complete Timed Research to encounter Rayquaza and more!
⚡ Ho-Oh, Gimmighoul holding a 10th anniversary coin, and more await in GO Pass: July!
⚡ The new event calendar takes your Pokémon GO adventures to the next level!
⚡ Introducing Scopely Explore
⚡ Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global final details!
⚡ Happy 10th anniversary to Pokémon GO!
⚡ Celebrate the launch of the Red Pokémon Jet: Take flight on a new adventure through the ANA and Pokémon GO partnership!
⚡ August 2026 Community Day: Nickit
⚡ Adventure awaits with the National Trust this summer!
⚡ Welcome to Pokémon GO Summer Quest
⚡ First partner and costumed Pokémon galore in Ultra Unlock: 10th Anniversary Edition!
⚡ Arrokuda and Cramorant splash into Pokémon GO during Ultra Unlock: Water Festival!
🤖 Google’s Demis Hassabis says it’s time for a global AI watchdog — led by the US
entry.summary: Demis Hassabis thinks the world needs an AI watchdog with the power to hit the brakes if frontier models become too dangerous. Writing in a blog post, the Google DeepMind CEO and cofounder said the US should lead the initiative, arguing that the country is the best place to set global standards "giv

entry.title: 🤖 The first sunlight reflecting space mirror has been cleared for launch
entry.summary: Reflect Orbital has been given the green light to launch its first space mirror that aims to redirect sunlight down to Earth at night. The US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has authorized the California-based startup to build and operate a single prototype satellite in low-Earth orbit later

entry.title: 🤖 New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
entry.summary: New hyperscale data centers can't set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation's first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state legislature that could restrict even more developments still awaits her signature. The order blocks new env

entry.title: 🤖 How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
entry.summary: See how data science teams can use ChatGPT Work to build root-cause briefs, impact readouts, KPI memos, scoped analyses, and dashboard specs from real work inputs.

entry.title: 🤖 How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
entry.summary: See how sales teams can use ChatGPT Work to create pipeline briefs, meeting prep packets, forecast reviews, account plans, and stalled-deal diagnoses from real work inputs.

entry.title: 🤖 Microsoft tests Windows Search without all the ads and fluff
entry.summary: Microsoft is testing a cleaner version of the Windows 11 search menu that strips it of recommended content and ads. In a blog post on Monday, Microsoft announced that it's rolling out the decluttered Search Box to Windows Insiders in the Experimental channel as the company looks to regain trust with

entry.title: 🤖 OnePlus is reportedly bailing on the US
entry.summary: OnePlus and its parent company, Oppo, plan to announce in the coming days that OnePlus brand will be leaving the US and European markets, according to a machine translation of a WinFuture report. Should the exit actually happen, it will mark a conclusion to months of rumors about the future of OnePl

entry.title: 🤖 Apple’s public betas for iOS 27 and more are out now
entry.summary: Apple has just released public betas for iOS 27 and other major OS updates that are set to publicly launch this fall. The big new feature this year is Siri AI, the delayed AI-powered revamp to Siri. It actually works - which is big praise! - though it keeps things brief. Other betas available now […

entry.title: 🤖 How MIT students are helping to prevent cyberattacks
entry.summary: Students from the MIT Cybersecurity Clinic help local governments and other vulnerable organizations defend against digital threats.

entry.title: 🤖 AI agents create virtual playgrounds to help robots get crucial training data
entry.summary: “SceneSmith” system uses collaborative AI agents to create realistic 3D environments of places like kitchens, hotels, and living rooms, where robots can simulate everyday chores.

entry.title: 🤖 New method aims to keep kids safe from illegal AI-generated content
entry.summary: Researchers developed an auditing technique to test generative AI models for malicious capabilities, without prompting them for illegal outputs.

entry.title: 🤖 How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
entry.summary: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

entry.title: 🤖 Getting started with ChatGPT
entry.summary: Learn how to use ChatGPT, start your first conversation, and discover simple ways to write, brainstorm, and solve problems with AI.

entry.title: 🤖 Tiny robot boats build floating structures
entry.summary: MIT researchers developed FloatForm, a swarm of small aquatic robots that snap together like ants forming a raft, assembling into reconfigurable structures on the water.

entry.title: 🤖 GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
entry.summary: Learn how GPT-5.6 powers Microsoft 365 Copilot with stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality work.

entry.title: 🤖 How novice coders can develop AI programs for military applications
entry.summary: A USAF cadet and a Lincoln Laboratory researcher found AI chatbots can help nontechnical service members produce viable software applications for their unique problems.

entry.title: 🤖 Jesse Thaler named director of the Laboratory for Nuclear Science
entry.summary: The professor of physics and inaugural director of the NSF AI Institute for Artificial Intelligence and Fundamental Interactions will lead LNS and continue his research in particle physics.

entry.title: 🤖 Toward a future that preserves benefits of neurotechnology for all
entry.summary: PhD student Rachel Sava, winner of the Envisioning the Future of Computing Prize, explores transformative improvements and dystopian risks of neural technology.

entry.title: 🤖 MIT in the media: Innovating and educating for the next 250 years of America
entry.summary: During a "Washington Post Live" panel discussion with ASU President Michael Crow, President Sally Kornbluth explored how universities are preparing the next generation of scientists to lead in America’s rapidly changing technological landscape.

entry.title: 🏆 Release Notes June 2026
🏆 THE ADIDAS STARR CUP IS ON!
🏆 Brawl Stars Challengers Finals: Tickets Available Now!
🏆 THE FORMULA BRAWL EVENT IS HERE!
🤖 Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
entry.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer conference , Google a

entry.title: 🏆 Brawl Cup Event Guide
🏆 Release Notes April 2026
🤖 Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
entry.summary: Railway , a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the limitations of leg

entry.title: 🤖 Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
entry.summary: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code , Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $20 to $200 per month

entry.title: 🤖 Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
entry.summary: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs , needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a billboard in San Francisco displaying what looked like

entry.title: 🤖 Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
entry.summary: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot , the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise data, drafting documents, and taking action on behalf

entry.title: 🤖 Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
entry.summary: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and a half, largely using Claude Code itself. The laun

entry.title: 🤖 Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
entry.summary: Nous Research , the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm , released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four days using 48 of Nvidia's latest B200 graphics pro
